content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-high

## Summary

All three amendments identify real correctness or robustness issues in the new prompt-context path. The strongest is the unnecessary `br list` dependency when `--prompt-file` is provided; the other two are smaller but valid data-quality defects in planned-elsewhere context generation.

## Votes

- `fr-10-eb47f625`: Accept
  `build_planned_elsewhere_context` adds downstream dependents first and then deduplicates later additions by bead id. If the same bead also appears in `shared_acceptance_owners`, the acceptance-criteria relationship text is lost with no explicit precedence rule. That is a real information-loss bug in prompt construction and it is better either to merge the relationship metadata or to explicitly filter dependents and document the precedence instead of relying on silent dedup side effects.
- `fr-10-265dc6c6`: Accept
  This is a genuine regression. `handle_create_from_bead` eagerly loads bead summaries and planned-elsewhere context before project creation, but `create_project_from_bead_context` ignores that generated prompt whenever `prompt_override` is present. As written, `project create-from-bead --prompt-file ...` can now fail on `br list` or prompt-hydration prerequisites that are irrelevant to the override path. The override path should bypass or degrade those lookups so prompt override remains isolated from unrelated summary-generation failures.
- `fr-10-88fbd81a`: Accept
  The current heading-skip helper only treats lines with at least two leading `#` characters as headings, so a valid leading `# Heading` is emitted as the summary text. That produces visibly degraded prompt context (`Summary: # Heading`) instead of the first real content line. Expanding the compaction logic to ignore level-1 ATX headings is a correct, low-risk fix that makes the summarizer behave consistently across standard markdown heading levels.
